text: A mushroom the size of a stool clears its throat. 'I'll trade you a secret for a secret,' it says. 'That's the economy out here.'

outcomes.text: You whisper yours. It whispers back the location of a buried coin cache. Fungal intel is shockingly reliable.
Your secret is now known to the entire mycelial network. Every mushroom for miles is judging you.

choices.text: Ask why a mushroom needs secrets

outcomes.text: 'Leverage,' it says instantly. You have no follow-up questions. You have learned something anyway.
It tastes like betrayal and umami. The forest goes very quiet. You feel amazing and hunted.
It was ready for this. It is, in fact, mildly toxic out of spite alone.

text: A bear blocks the path, holding a piece of paper upside down. It growls, then, haltingly: 'You... read?' It appears to be a summons.

choices.text: Read the summons for the bear

success.text: The bear is being evicted from its own cave by a beaver holding company. You explain. The bear nods grimly and pays you in salmon and one shiny rock.

fail.text: You misread 'eviction' as 'invitation.' The bear attends the beaver gala. It does not go well. The bear blames you, physically.

outcomes.text: The bear watches you go, paper still upside down, somehow disappointed in the entire literate world.

success.text: You win on a technicality: beavers can't notarize. The forest pays you in prestige and buried acorn-gold.

fail.text: You lose to a beaver in a tiny waistcoat. The bear's look of betrayal will haunt you longer than the bruises.

text: A bush of plump berries, beside a hand-painted sign: 'DEFINITELY SAFE BERRIES.' The paint is fresh. The handwriting is nervous.

success.text: Sweet, harmless, delightful. The sign was telling the truth, which is somehow the biggest twist of your day.

fail.text: The sign lied. Your tongue goes numb and your shadow looks wrong for an hour.

outcomes.text: One berry's worth of poison is just a tingle. One berry's worth of delicious is still delicious. Science wins again, in miniature.

success.text: You find a goblin with purple-stained hands and zero remorse. He bribes you with real, verified rations to keep quiet.

fail.text: The trail leads in a circle back to the sign, which you'd swear now reads 'DEFINITELY SAFE BERRIES :)'.

text: A hut on impressively muscular chicken legs settles down in front of you, blocking the path with intent. A witch leans out. 'Well? I don't stop for just anyone. The hut liked you.'

outcomes.text: Herbs, chanting, a soup that tastes like being twelve again. You leave whole and smelling faintly of thyme.
The witch and the hut exchange a look. 'Corn,' says the witch, in the exact tone of a lie. The hut licks its doorstep. You've learned something you can't unlearn.

success.text: The hut does a small, devastating curtsy. The witch, disarmed, gives you a jar of something restorative 'for the road, and because it flexed.'

fail.text: The hut takes it wrong and does a warning stomp. The resulting mud wave is personal.

text: A perfect circle of mushrooms in a clearing. Every childhood story you know screams in unison. The circle smells like rain and bad decisions.

outcomes.text: The world hiccups. You are somewhere else in the forest, three hours older, holding a flower you don't remember picking. Fair trade.
Applause from nowhere. Something appreciated your confidence and left a tip.
You are ejected from the circle by consensus. The bruise is shaped like disapproval.

choices.text: Leave an offering at the edge

outcomes.text: The coins sink into the moss with a sound like a purr. Your path out of the forest is inexplicably shorter and all downhill.
You monster. Delicious, delicious monster. The forest disapproves; your stomach files a dissent.
The mushrooms scream. Softly, but they scream. You stop immediately and pretend it never happened. The forest pretends with you.

text: A goblin scooter lies crashed against a stump, wheels still spinning. Its rider is gone. A note pinned to the seat: 'BACK IN FIVE MINUTES. DO NOT TOUCH. -DEATHWHEEL THE UNSTOPPABLE'. The note is weathered by months of rain.

success.text: Bent forks, loose chain, mysterious lever that does nothing. An hour's work. Deathwheel's loss is your commute.

fail.text: The mysterious lever did something after all. The scooter, briefly, flies. Then teaches you why it shouldn't.

outcomes.text: You wait ten, out of courtesy. Deathwheel does not return. Deathwheel has not returned for some time, the moss implies. You leave a small stone as a memorial and feel briefly, unaccountably noble.
Chain, bell, one good wheel, and a saddlebag containing Deathwheel's emergency stash. The forest reclaims the rest.

text: A beehive the size of a wardrobe hums in the crook of an oak. The bees seem organized. Suspiciously organized. There are little guard rotations.

success.text: In, out, one sting as a processing fee. The comb is heavy and golden and worth every welt.

fail.text: The guard rotation was for exactly this. The swarm escorts you a full quarter mile with prejudice.

choices.text: Leave an offering of your rations

outcomes.text: The bees inspect the trade, confer, and approve. Comb is lowered to you on a strand of wax like a tiny crane delivery. Commerce.
An hour of watching reveals shift changes, a dance-based bureaucracy, and what is unmistakably a customs checkpoint. You take notes. Scholars will call you mad. Scholars weren't here.
